approval_matrix.description: Defines the hierarchy of approval based on monetary thresholds, mapping amount ranges to specific roles and individuals.
approval_matrix.business_context: Used to verify which person or role is required to approve an invoice or purchase order of a specific value.

id.description: Unique identifier for the approval rule.

min_amount.description: The lower bound of the amount range for this approval level.

max_amount.description: The upper bound of the amount range for this approval level.

approver_role.description: The job title or role responsible for approval at this level.

approver_name.description: The specific name of the person authorized to approve.

approver_email.description: Contact email of the authorized approver.

companies.description: Internal legal entities or branch offices belonging to the parent organization.
companies.business_context: Identifies which internal company is the purchaser or the owner of a transaction.

name.description: Registered legal name of the entity.

gstin.description: Goods and Services Tax Identification Number.

state_code.description: Standard numerical code for the state of registration.

is_buyer.description: Flag indicating if this entity acts as a purchasing unit.

departments.description: Functional units within a company responsible for specific business operations.
departments.business_context: Used to track spending, budgets, and ownership of purchase orders by specific teams.

company_id.description: The ID of the company this department belongs to.

name.description: Name of the department (e.g., Engineering, Marketing).

head_name.description: Name of the person leading the department.

budget_annual.description: Total allocated spending limit for the department for the year.

relationships.description: Links a department to its parent company entity.

grn_line_items.description: Detailed line-by-line records of specific products received in a Good Receipt Note.
grn_line_items.business_context: Used to check for quantity discrepancies between what was ordered and what was actually delivered.

quantity_received.description: The amount of product actually accepted into the warehouse.

quantity_rejected.description: The amount of product returned due to damage or quality issues.

relationships.description: Links the line item to the main GRN header.
Identifies the specific product being received.
Maps the received quantity back to the original order line for reconciliation.

grns.description: Goods Receipt Notes (GRN) representing the official acknowledgement of goods received from a vendor.
grns.business_context: A critical step in the 'Three-Way Match' (PO vs GRN vs Invoice) to ensure the company only pays for what it received.

grn_number.description: Unique document number for the receipt.

grn_date.description: The date when the goods were delivered and processed.

status.description: The current state of the receipt processing.

relationships.description: Identifies the supplier who delivered the goods.
Links the receipt to the specific purchase order it fulfills.

invoice_line_items.description: Specific line items listed on a vendor's invoice.
invoice_line_items.business_context: Used to audit individual charges, tax breakdowns (GST), and unit rates on a bill.

total_amount.description: The taxable value of this line item before tax.

cgst.description: Central Goods and Services Tax amount.

sgst.description: State Goods and Services Tax amount.

igst.description: Integrated Goods and Services Tax amount (used for inter-state trades).

relationships.description: Links the item to the parent invoice header.
Links the line to a specific product catalog entry.

invoices.description: Vendor-issued bills requesting payment for goods or services.
invoices.business_context: Core table for accounts payable; tracks how much is owed, when it's due, and current approval/payment status.

invoice_number.description: The unique reference number provided by the vendor.

grand_total.description: The final amount to be paid including all taxes.

status.description: The lifecycle stage of the invoice.

deviation_type.description: Categorization of discrepancies found during validation (e.g., price mismatch).

relationships.description: The supplier who issued the bill.
The internal entity that must pay the bill.
The order against which this invoice was raised.

payments.description: Records of actual cash outflows or transfers to fulfill invoices.
payments.business_context: Used to track actual cash flow and verify when an invoice has been cleared.

amount.description: The specific amount paid in this transaction.

payment_mode.description: The banking method used for transfer.

relationships.description: The bill that is being settled by this payment.

po_line_items.description: Individual items requested within a Purchase Order.
po_line_items.business_context: Used to see exactly what was ordered and at what agreed-upon price.

total_amount.description: Quantity multiplied by rate for this line.

relationships.description: Links the detail line to the main order.
Identifies the catalog product being ordered.

products.description: Master list of goods and services that the company procures.
products.business_context: Central source of truth for standard rates and classifications (categories).

category.description: High-level grouping of products (e.g., IT, Stationery).

standard_rate.description: The expected price per unit for this product.

purchase_orders.description: Official legal contracts issued to vendors specifying items to be purchased.
purchase_orders.business_context: Used to track commitments and planned spend before an invoice arrives.

enum_values.approved: Order ready to be sent to vendor

relationships.description: The department that initiated the request.
The supplier chosen for this order.
The entity paying for the order.

vendors.description: Master data for external suppliers and service providers.
vendors.business_context: Used for vendor performance analysis, risk tracking (watchlist), and payment term management.

rating.description: Internal quality or reliability grade (A, B, C).

is_watchlist.description: Flag for vendors with known issues or compliance risks.

payment_terms_days.description: The number of days allowed between invoice date and payment date.

payment_to_vendor.description: Trace a payment back to the vendor through the invoice.
payment_to_vendor.use_when: Who did we pay this month?

po_to_department.description: Check which department raised an order.
po_to_department.use_when: What is the total order value for the Engineering department?

invoice_reconciliation_path.description: Join invoices to their parent POs and the resulting receipts (GRNs).
invoice_reconciliation_path.use_when: Compare invoice amounts with what was ordered and received.

total_liability.description: Total amount due to vendors for invoices not yet paid.

avg_payment_delay.description: Average days taken to pay an invoice beyond its due date.

rejection_rate.description: Percentage of items rejected by the warehouse due to issues.

active_vendor_count.description: Number of unique vendors we have ordered from recently.

last_quarter.sql_template: CAST((strftime('%m', {col}) - 1) / 3 + 1 AS INT) = CAST((strftime('%m', 'now', '-3 months') - 1) / 3 + 1 AS INT) AND strftime('%Y', {col}) = strftime('%Y', 'now', '-3 months')

date_clarification.clarification_question: Are you asking about when the invoice was issued, approved, or when it is due?

amount_clarification.clarification_question: Do you want the total including taxes or just the base taxable amount?

window_functions.name: vendor_spending_rank
window_functions.description: Rank vendors based on total business volume.
window_functions.template: SELECT vendor_id, SUM(grand_total) as volume, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(grand_total) DESC) as rnk FROM invoices GROUP BY 1

window_functions.name: payment_running_total
window_functions.template: SELECT payment_date, amount, SUM(amount) OVER (ORDER BY payment_date) as cumulative FROM payments

common_patterns.name: three_way_match_check
common_patterns.description: Compare PO, GRN, and Invoice quantities to find mismatches.
common_patterns.template: SELECT po.po_number, po_li.quantity as ordered, grn_li.quantity_received as received, inv_li.quantity as billed FROM purchase_orders po JOIN po_line_items po_li ON po.id = po_li.po_id LEFT JOIN grn_line_items grn_li ON po_li.id = grn_li.po_line_item_id LEFT JOIN invoice_line_items inv_li ON po_li.product_id = inv_li.product_id AND inv_li.invoice_id IN (SELECT id FROM invoices WHERE po_id = po.id)
